title: AI Governance and Risk Assessment Pack

intake_questions.text: Has the organisation deployed AI or ML systems in production?
Does the organisation process personal data through AI systems?
Is there a documented AI governance policy or AI ethics framework?
Have Data Protection Impact Assessments been conducted for AI processing of personal data?
Are AI model decisions subject to human review for consequential outcomes?

scope_template.prompt: Identify AI/ML systems and use cases in scope for this governance assessment
List AI models, data pipelines, and AI-powered products included in scope
Confirm AI systems or use cases explicitly excluded from this assessment

requirements.text: An AI governance framework aligned to TGB-AI-01 must be established including an AI system inventory, risk classification, accountable owner per system, and board-level oversight.
requirements.evidence_expectation: AI governance policy; AI system inventory with risk classification; AI owner register; board/committee oversight evidence

requirements.text: AI systems classified as high-risk must have completed a risk assessment covering bias, explainability, data quality, adversarial robustness, and privacy impact.
requirements.evidence_expectation: AI risk assessment records; bias testing results; model cards; explainability documentation; DPIA for personal data processing

requirements.text: High-risk AI decisions must incorporate meaningful human oversight including ability to override, audit trail of AI recommendations vs human decisions, and escalation paths.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Human-in-the-loop process documentation; override audit logs; escalation procedure; sample decision records

requirements.text: Training data used for AI systems must be documented for provenance, completeness, bias assessment, and data subject rights compliance under GDPR/DPDP.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Training data register; data provenance records; bias audit results; data subject rights assessment for training data

requirements.text: Deployed AI systems must be continuously monitored for model drift, performance degradation, bias amplification, and adversarial inputs, with alerts and retraining triggers documented.
requirements.evidence_expectation: Model monitoring configuration; drift detection evidence; performance dashboards; retraining trigger documentation; incident log

evidence_requests.title: AI Governance Framework and System Inventory
evidence_requests.description: AI governance policy, system inventory with risk classifications, owner register, and board oversight evidence

evidence_requests.title: AI Risk Assessment Records
evidence_requests.description: Risk assessments and model cards for high-risk AI systems; bias testing results; DPIAs for personal data processing

evidence_requests.title: Human Oversight Process Documentation
evidence_requests.description: Human-in-the-loop procedures, override capability evidence, decision audit logs, and escalation paths

evidence_requests.title: Training Data Governance Records
evidence_requests.description: Training data register with provenance, bias assessment results, and data subject rights compliance evidence

evidence_requests.title: AI Model Monitoring Evidence
evidence_requests.description: Monitoring configuration, drift detection evidence, performance dashboards, and retraining trigger documentation

task_templates.title: Review AI governance framework and system inventory completeness
Assess risk classification methodology for AI systems
Evaluate human oversight mechanisms for high-risk AI decisions
Review training data governance and bias assessment records
Assess AI model monitoring and incident response capability
Interview AI/ML engineering and data science teams on model development lifecycle
Map AI systems against EU AI Act risk categories for future compliance readiness

finding_templates.title_pattern: High-risk AI system missing required assessment: {assessment_type}
No human override capability for AI decision type: {decision_type}

severity_model.critical: High-risk AI system operating with no governance, no oversight, or processing personal data without DPIA; immediate regulatory risk
severity_model.high: Significant AI governance gap; high-risk system without bias assessment, human oversight, or monitoring
severity_model.medium: Partial AI governance implementation; inventory incomplete or risk classification inconsistently applied
severity_model.low: Minor AI governance gap; documentation or monitoring enhancement needed; low-risk system without formal assessment
severity_model.informational: AI governance maturity enhancement recommendation; no immediate risk

advisory_clinic_templates.title: AI Inventory and Classification Workshop
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Facilitate discovery of AI systems in use; apply risk classification criteria; assign accountability per system

advisory_clinic_templates.title: AI DPIA Scoping Session
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Identify which AI systems require DPIA under GDPR; scope the assessment; advise on bias testing approach

advisory_clinic_templates.title: Human-in-the-Loop Design Advisory
advisory_clinic_templates.guidance: Review AI decision workflows; advise on override mechanism design; define audit trail requirements for AI decisions
